item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What? Why?.. Take my vote and go away. +1

item.title: Squeeze Play
item.content:encoded:
In standard American-rules baseball, a squeeze play is one answer to having a runner on third and a player up to bat. The at-bat player makes a sacrifice bunt — that's a bunt when the strike count is less than two, since the pitcher can retrieve the ball and throw it to first base before the runner gets there.

Now, because it's a bunt, the pitcher will have to leave the mound to get the ball. Once he has it, he has a fraction of a second to decide whether he's throwing to first, to stop the man who bunted, or to home plate, to stop the runner from third. Hesitate too long and your opponent scores a run batted in AND has a runner on first. Throw to first and the run scores. Throw to home and the runner is on base. Unless the pitcher is very fast and very lucky, the other team gets some advantage.

Because of this, "squeeze play" also refers to a situation in which you pressure someone to give you something — you're "squeezing" them to get what you want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even after reading the explanation, I still have only a vague idea of what it's about, but it seems also to be a completely uncontained cognito-hazard? I feel like the foundation wouldn't be so sloppy as to just allow the thing to infect people via its own page.

I also just really don't get it and it flies far over my head

-1 for now, it just doesn't do literally anything for me. Open to an explanation of what a squeeze play is? I'm just very lost here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This went way over my head. I somehow got the feeling that I was reading a cognito-hazard but couldn't tell how or why, and for sure can't explain how there's a deity involved. Not sure about a +1. I feel it's missing a few pieces to fully revealing this entire page is a trick. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to admit that zero is indeed the proper number of wings for an elephant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I figured it would do well to post an explanation of this SCP for anyone who's having trouble getting it, past/present/future.

SPOILERS
This is copy+pasted from a chat so it could be formatted better.

it's some deity/superhuman force/entity/thing that took its original form on earth as a program that described images and provided judgments

the article was written by people affected by the program to be a decoy, so it understates the nature of the anomaly drastically while exposing the reader to the ultimate 'squeeze play' judgment

  • the first judgment implies that the 'inaccuracies' are not actually inaccurate, and that there's reasoning behind the judgments
  • second one states that, at least in their view, it's the photos themselves that are inaccurate, and 2926 describes what they should be
  • third one can find out what people agree to be the nature of images fed into 2926, and modify them to match what 2926 thinks they should be
  • fourth one compels people to basically arrange a situation (you reading the article) where the right person views the 'squeeze play' judgment
  • fifth one coordinates all of the above to create a consensus reality (what people agree to be real) in which 2926 is capable of manifesting as "the sphinx"
  • sixth one creates attendants to the sphinx, and this is where it shifts into present tense because they're gonna do this in just a second once you become the sphinx
  • seventh one states that the sphinx is realized via you, because you have no inaccuracies, and 'the situation is immanent' which i guess means that you were the anomaly, or something like that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few lines were ones that stuck with me a while after reading them and came to mind when I was trying to think of easter eggs, a few were just the stingers from ones that came up when I hit Random SCP/Tale. I forget which are which.

Basically, there is no significance beyond being easter eggs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Curse your usage of obscure words that sound like less-obscure words! I like this a lot though.

What's really bugging me is the significance of the objects and tales you've taken the judgments from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this because I am the sphinx. I am a great favorite. I will never die.

I do like that you unexpunged the final bit. Without it, it just doesn't feel complete. And this is coming from a serial expunger. Really, it's an interesting little piece. Weird, which is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It must be a coincidence, because I've never read Illuminatus!. The stuff about the 'sphinx' felt appropriate, seeing as how 2926 is the heavy judger of men.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels very ophite to me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mildly interesting, but ultimately there's no real emotional or intellectual impact.

Edit: I do dig the Illuminatus! reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Afterwards, but also at three in the morning, which might be a contributing factor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you read it before or after I unexpunged the final bit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems to be building up into some greater whole that never arrives (or, perhaps, that I sinply missed) but each of the components works well enough to garner an up vote from me.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apparently not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The reader, because they read the 'squeeze play' judgment, becomes the receptacle for 2926 AKA the sphinx. Was that not obvious?

EDIT: I wrote up a little snippet for the payoff. Does it work better now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wanted to like this, but you expunge the big payoff. It's a total buzzkill for a weird concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cliche creepypasta bullshit that we don't need on the site? Maybe.

Properly reviewed and edited for quality? lolno.

Fun as hell to write? I'd say so.

I feel so empty.
I'm not the tooth fairy.
It grew to the size of an orange.
These are strange times.
God, wouldn't it be beautiful?
I think I broke it.
A squeeze play that works two ways.

Image licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 3.0 Unported license by Yathin S Krishnappa.

"Immanent" is not a typo.
